title: Ulmus campestris lusus major
taxonomy: Ulmus campestris lusus major is considered a subspecies of the field elm tree, classified within the genus Ulmus and family Ulmaceae. It is a variant of the field elm tree, which is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The field elm tree is classified as Ulmus minor in many botanical sources, with some considering Ulmus campestris lusus major as a regional variation. However, the exact classification of Ulmus campestris lusus major may vary depending on the source and regional context. The International Plant Names Index recognizes Ulmus campestris, but does not specifically list Ulmus campestris lusus major.
uses: Ulmus campestris lusus major, which is a variant of the field elm, is often used in traditional medicine due to its purported health benefits. Its bark, leaves, and twigs have been employed in folk remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In addition, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using Ulmus campestris lusus major for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Ulmus campestris lusus major, a variant of the field elm, is not well-documented in terms of its health benefits. properties: Ulmus campestris lusus major, also known as the elm tree, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark and leaves believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The extracts from the tree have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. constituents: Ulmus campestris lusus major is a variant of the field elm tree, but specific information on its active constituents is limited. parts: Ulmus campestris lusus major, also known as the large-fruited field elm, has been reported to have various medicinal properties. The bark of Ulmus campestris lusus major is used in traditional medicine due to its astringent and antiseptic properties. It is believed to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. The plant's extracts are also used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. preparations: Ulmus campestris lusus major, also known as the Wych Elm, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. The bark and leaves of the tree are used to prepare various herbal remedies, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
